homework.title: Textual Analysis of 'My Childhood'
homework.instructions: Read the text 'My Childhood' carefully. Identify three specific instances where the author uses descriptive language to evoke a sense of nostalgia or childhood innocence. Quote these lines and explain how they contribute to the overall theme.
homework.question: Select three passages from the text that best illustrate the author's childhood environment. For each passage, write a short paragraph (50-70 words) explaining the literary device used and its effect on the reader.
homework.expectedResponse: The student should identify specific quotes related to childhood settings or feelings, correctly name a literary device (e.g., imagery, metaphor), and explain its impact on the theme of nostalgia.

homework.title: Reflective Essay: My Own Childhood
homework.instructions: Write a reflective essay (300-400 words) titled 'My Childhood'. Draw inspiration from the style and themes of 'My Childhood' but focus on your own experiences. Include specific details about a place, a person, or an event that shaped your early years.
homework.question: Compose a descriptive narrative essay reflecting on your childhood. Ensure you include a clear introduction, body paragraphs with specific examples, and a concluding reflection on how these memories influence you today.
homework.expectedResponse: A well-structured essay demonstrating the ability to recall specific memories, use descriptive language effectively, and reflect on personal growth or values derived from childhood experiences.

homework.title: Word Usage and Synonyms
homework.instructions: From the text 'My Childhood', select five words that are central to the theme of childhood or nostalgia. For each word, write a new sentence that uses the word in a different context than it appears in the text.
homework.question: List 5 key vocabulary words from the chapter. For each word, provide: 1) The meaning as used in the text, 2) A synonym, and 3) A new sentence using the word in a different context.
homework.expectedResponse: Accurate identification of vocabulary words, correct synonyms, and creative usage of those words in new contexts demonstrating understanding beyond the text.

homework.title: Comparing Childhood Eras
homework.instructions: Compare the childhood experiences described in 'My Childhood' with those of children today. Create a Venn diagram (drawn on paper) or a comparison table highlighting at least three similarities and three differences regarding play, education, or family life.
homework.question: Analyze the text to identify aspects of the author's childhood. Compare these with your own or modern childhood experiences. Present your findings in a structured format (table or list) covering: 1) Play activities, 2) Educational methods, 3) Family interactions.
homework.visualRequirement: Draw a Venn diagram or create a comparison table showing similarities and differences.
homework.expectedResponse: A logical comparison identifying valid similarities and differences between the historical context of the text and modern life, demonstrating critical thinking about social changes.

homework.title: Family Story Interview
homework.instructions: Interview a parent or grandparent about their childhood. Ask them about one specific game they played or a tradition they followed. Write down three key points from their story and share one interesting fact with the class.
homework.question: Conduct a short interview with an elder family member about their childhood. Record three interesting details they shared. In your homework notebook, write a brief summary (100 words) of what you learned from this conversation.
homework.expectedResponse: A concise summary demonstrating active listening and the ability to synthesize oral information into written text.
